track.title: Global Stocks and ETFs
track.description: A starter route for global investing from Kenya: ETFs, FX, platform checks, custody, fees, and tax questions.

course.title: Global Stocks and ETFs: Core route
course.description: Understand global routes before using foreign platforms.

lessons.title: What is an ETF?
lessons.summary: An ETF can give basket exposure, but the route still has platform, market, and currency risk.
lessons.introduction: An exchange-traded fund usually gives exposure to a basket of assets through a market-traded instrument. For a Kenyan beginner, the route matters as much as the asset: platform, currency, custody, fees, and tax questions.
sections: ETF does not mean simple or suitable for every goal.
Global access can add foreign exchange costs and account restrictions.
A basket can reduce single-company exposure but does not remove market risk.

scenario.title: US ETF curiosity
scenario.text: A user wants a US ETF after seeing online posts. They first list FX spread, platform fees, custody model, and withdrawal route.

lessons.action: Write the exact route: KES to foreign currency, platform, custody, fees, and withdrawal.
lessons.takeaway: Global education starts with route safety, not product excitement.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify any platform, tax, and legal questions with official/provider/professional sources.

lessons.title: USD/KES currency risk
lessons.summary: Currency movement can change the KES result even when the foreign asset moves differently.
lessons.introduction: When money moves between KES and another currency, exchange rates affect the outcome. A foreign investment can gain in its own currency while the KES result still depends on conversion rates and fees.
sections: FX spread is a cost that may appear at deposit, conversion, or withdrawal.
Currency movement can help or hurt the KES value.
Diaspora users should separate home-currency goals from Kenya-currency goals.

scenario.title: KES school-fee goal
scenario.text: A user investing in USD has a KES school-fee goal. They track both market result and exchange-rate movement.

lessons.mistake: Ignoring FX cost because the app shows a clean foreign-currency balance.
lessons.action: Estimate deposit FX cost, withdrawal FX cost, and the currency you need at the goal date.
lessons.takeaway: Currency is part of the route, not a footnote.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Use provider disclosures and professional tax/financial review where needed.

lessons.title: Platform and custody risk
lessons.summary: Know who holds assets, what jurisdiction applies, and how account access works.
lessons.introduction: A platform is not only a user interface. It is part of the legal, custody, funding, withdrawal, and support route. Beginners should know what happens if the platform restricts accounts or changes access.
sections: Check regulatory status and terms in the relevant jurisdiction.
Ask how assets are held and what investor protection exists, if any.
Understand deposit, transfer, and withdrawal routes before sending money.

scenario.title: Account restriction
scenario.text: A user opens a foreign platform account and later faces a withdrawal review. They wish they had understood documentation requirements earlier.

lessons.mistake: Treating a popular app as proof of safety.
lessons.action: Write platform regulator, custody explanation, deposit route, and withdrawal route.
lessons.takeaway: The platform is part of the risk.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify platform terms and licensing directly; PesaRoute does not endorse providers.

lessons.title: Global route simulator practice
lessons.summary: Use the simulator to practice FX fees, transfer costs, and USD amount estimates.
lessons.introduction: The global route simulator helps users see how KES amount, exchange rate, FX spread, transfer fees, and platform fees can affect estimated foreign-currency exposure.
sections: Change FX spread and see how much less reaches the investment account.
Change transfer fee assumptions and compare small vs larger amounts.
Record assumptions before interpreting the estimate.

scenario.title: KES 50,000 global route
scenario.text: A user estimates how much USD exposure remains after transfer and FX costs.

lessons.mistake: Assuming the full KES amount becomes invested exposure.
lessons.action: Run one global route simulation and save one assumption to your journal.
lessons.takeaway: Fees and FX can make the route very different from the headline amount.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify actual fees and tax questions with platform/provider/professional sources.

quiz_questions.prompt: What should be checked about a global platform?
quiz_questions.explanation: The platform is part of the risk and route.

quiz_questions.prompt: What can affect the KES result?
quiz_questions.explanation: Currency conversion is part of the route.

quiz_questions.prompt: What is the global simulator for?

flashcards.back: A market-traded fund that usually gives basket exposure.
flashcards.example: A US ETF may track a broad index.

flashcards.back: Exchange-rate movement changing the result when converting currencies.
flashcards.example: USD result and KES result can differ.

flashcards.back: Uncertainty about how assets are held and protected.
The difference between buy and sell conversion rates or market reference rates.
flashcards.example: It can reduce money reaching the account.

flashcards.back: A route is incomplete if you do not know how money comes back.
flashcards.example: Bank transfer limits or reviews may apply.

resources.title: Global investing from Kenya checklist
resources.body: A checklist for global investing routes: platform status, custody, FX cost, transfer fees, tax questions, withdrawal route, account restrictions, and documentation.
items: Ask tax questions before assuming net returns.
